2026-03-25: Complete (3) Hard Tests on General Word Problems. Focus on translating English into clean algebraic equations. If you hit 1/3 completed, push through the remaining two — hard tests are where the score improvements live.
2026-03-26: Review your Mistakes + Read 'How to Get Faster at GMAT Verbal' + Complete Review Quiz 6. Today is a process day: analyze WHY each mistake happened, not just that it was wrong. Pattern recognition in errors is everything.
2026-03-27: Read Unit Conversions (TTP Lesson). Focus on conversion chains — multi-step problems where you must track units at every step. Write out the unit labels as you work so nothing slips through.
2026-03-28: Heavy test day: Complete (2) Easy, (1) Medium, (1) Hard Unit Conversion tests + 6 more tasks. Pace yourself across the day. Review after each test set before moving on.
2026-03-29: Rest day — but stay sharp. Take 20 minutes to re-read your mistake log from the week. Let the patterns marinate.
2026-03-30: Review your Mistakes + Read 'Talk Yourself Into Flow to Optimize Your Performance' + Complete Review Quiz 7 (target accuracy: 75%). Mindset work is not optional — it's strategy.
2026-03-31: Read Rate Problems — Lessons 9.1 to 9.10.2. Core formula: Distance = Rate × Time. When two objects move toward each other, add the rates. Don't overcomplicate it.
2026-04-01: Read Rate Problems — Lessons 9.10.3 to 9.15. Focus on converging/diverging motion problems and rate problems with rest intervals. Draw timelines when the scenario is complex.
2026-04-02: Read Rate Problems — Lessons 9.16 to 9.20. Push through — you're in the final stretch of Rate Problems reading. These are testable and frequently appear on the GMAT.
2026-04-03: Read Rate Problems — Lessons 9.20.1 to the end. Final reading day for this topic. Lock in the concepts before the practice tests hit.
2026-04-04: Heavy test day: Complete (3) Medium + (2) Hard Rate Problems tests + 5 more tasks (7h 34m total). This is a long day. Break it into blocks. Review every single mistake before the next test set.
2026-04-05: Rest day. Light review only. Look back at Rate Problems mistakes and identify the 2-3 patterns that tripped you up most.
2026-04-06: Review your Mistakes + Read 'Momentum > Motivation' + Complete Review Quiz 8. The lesson is true: motivation fades, momentum carries you. Build yours today.
2026-04-07: Read Work Problems — Lessons 10.1 to 10.8. Key formula: combined rate = 1/a + 1/b. Two people/machines working together? Add their individual rates, not their times.
2026-04-08: Read Work Problems — Lessons 10.8.1 to 10.11. Focus on machines turning on/off mid-problem and partial completion scenarios. Set up equations before solving.
2026-04-09: Complete (2) Easy + (3) Medium Work Problem tests. First practice day on this topic. Aim for understanding over speed — accuracy locks in the pattern recognition.
2026-04-10: Complete (2) Hard Work Problem tests + Review your Mistakes. Hard tests are the ceiling-raisers. Review every error deeply today.
2026-04-11: Big day before the exam: Read Find the Conclusion (CR) + Complete (2) Easy, (2) Medium Find the Conclusion tests + 6 more tasks. This is your final prep push. Stay calm, stay sharp.
2026-04-12: 🎯 GMAT TEST DAY. You have prepared for this. Trust the work. Breathe. Read every question twice. Manage your time. You've got this, Jeena.
2026-04-13: Post-exam: Take the TTP Review Test + Complete Review Quiz 9. Reset your brain, assess where you stand, and get back on the horse. This score is just data — use it.
2026-04-14: Read Ratios — Lessons 11.1 to 11.9. Ratios express relationships between parts, not exact values. Always write a:b = kx:ky and solve for k when needed.
2026-04-15: Read Ratios — Lessons 11.10 to 11.15. Focus on multi-ratio problems that require a common term. Convert ratios to the same base before combining.
2026-04-16: Read Ratios — Lessons 11.15.1 to 11.17 + Complete (2) Easy Ratios tests. First practice on Ratios today. Focus on setting up the equation cleanly before calculating.
2026-04-17: Complete (4) Medium Ratios tests. This is where patterns lock in. Review every mistake and note whether it was a conceptual error or a careless one — they need different fixes.
2026-04-18: Complete (1) Hard Ratios test + Review your Mistakes + Read Evaluate the Argument (CR Lessons 9.1–9.6) + 7 more tasks. Heavy day. Evaluate the Argument CR questions ask: what additional information would help assess the argument?
2026-04-19: Rest day. Light review of your Ratios mistake log. Identify the top 2 ratio sub-types that still feel shaky.
2026-04-20: Read Percent Word Problems — Lessons 12.1 to 12.9. Core formula: percent change = (new − old) / old × 100. Watch for the trap: a 20% increase then 20% decrease ≠ original. You end up at 96%.
2026-04-21: Read Percent Word Problems — Lessons 12.10 to 12.13. Focus on successive percent change and percent of a percent problems. Write out each step clearly.
2026-04-22: Read Percent Word Problems — Lessons 12.14 to 12.16. Final reading day for this topic. Solidify the formulas and approach before practice begins.
2026-04-23: Complete (3) Easy Percent Word Problems tests. Accuracy day — build confidence with the material before moving to harder sets.
2026-04-24: Complete (5) Medium Percent Word Problems tests. Medium tests are where real GMAT score is built. Go deep on every mistake.
2026-04-25: Complete (1) Hard Percent Word Problems test + Review your Mistakes + Read Logical Flaw (CR Lessons 10.1–10.3) + 8 more tasks. Logical Flaw CR questions: identify the flawed reasoning — circular logic, false cause, sampling error, false analogy.
2026-04-26: Rest day. Review your Percent Word Problems errors and identify the top 2-3 mistake patterns. Note them in your mistake log.
2026-04-27: Read Overlapping Sets — Lessons 13.1 to 13.2.4. Use a two-circle Venn diagram or a matrix. Formula: Total = Group A + Group B − Both + Neither. Always check if 'neither' is in play.
2026-04-28: Read Overlapping Sets — Lessons 13.2.5 to 13.3.1. Focus on three-group overlapping sets — they require a more complex Venn diagram. Draw it out every time.
2026-04-29: Read Overlapping Sets — Lessons 13.3.2 to end + Complete (5) Easy + (2) Medium Overlapping Sets tests + 1 more. First practice day. Diagram every problem before solving.
2026-04-30: Review your Mistakes (1h). Dedicated error analysis day. Be ruthless. For each mistake, write one sentence about exactly what went wrong.
2026-05-01: Read Complete the Passage (RC) + Complete (2) Easy Complete the Passage tests. For Reading Comprehension, map the structure of the passage before answering. Know where to find information.
2026-05-02: Complete (2) Medium + (2) Hard Complete the Passage tests + Review your Mistakes + 5 more tasks (7h 35m). Heavy RC day. Read actively — track the author's argument and tone throughout.
